title: KryptonitePlan
description: Schema for a kryptonite implementation plan document. Generated during Phase 11 after spec approval. Validated by validate-plan.js before execution begins.

version.description: Semantic version of this plan document, e.g. 1.0.0

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when this plan was generated

epic_slug.description: Slug identifier for the epic this plan belongs to, e.g. user-auth

spec_version.description: Semantic version of the spec document this plan was generated from

total_stories.description: Total number of user stories covered by this plan

total_waves.description: Total number of execution waves in this plan

critical_path_depth.description: Length of the longest dependency chain through the plan

estimated_duration.description: Human-readable total duration estimate, e.g. '3-4 days'

waves.description: Ordered list of execution waves. Each wave is a cohesive unit of parallel work.

name.description: Short human-readable name for this wave

theme.description: Thematic description of what this wave accomplishes, e.g. 'Core authentication foundation'

estimated_duration.description: Human-readable duration estimate for this wave, e.g. '1-2 days'

prerequisites.description: IDs of waves that must complete before this wave begins. Omit or leave empty for wave-1.

parallel_groups.description: Groups of stories and tasks that can execute concurrently within this wave

id.description: Unique identifier for this parallel group, e.g. pg-1-a

domain.description: Technical domain this group touches, e.g. 'backend', 'frontend', 'database'

blocking.description: Whether other parallel groups in this wave must wait for this group to complete

stories.description: Story IDs covered by this parallel group

tasks.description: Concrete implementation tasks within this group

story_ref.description: The story this task implements, e.g. US-001

file_paths.description: Files this task creates or modifies

commands.description: Shell commands to run as part of this task (generators, migrations, etc.)

effort.description: Effort size estimate. S=small (<2h), M=medium (2-4h), L=large (4-8h), XL=extra-large (>8h)

depends_on.description: Task IDs that must complete before this task begins

description.description: What is demonstrated at this checkpoint

validates.description: Story IDs verified at this checkpoint

criteria.description: Specific pass/fail criteria for the demo checkpoint

shared_artifacts.description: Pre-dispatch reconciliation manifest. Each entry pins a model / type / enum / base-class / factory that two or more stories in this wave reference but that does NOT yet exist — so the stories don't each invent a different (incompatible) version of it. The owner_story creates it; everyone in reused_by must reuse, never re-create. The orchestrator dispatches the owner first (place it in a `blocking: true` parallel group — see references/execution-protocol.md 'blocking groups'), then fans out the reusers against the now-stable artifact. Distinct from shared_registry_files (existing files many stories append to): shared_artifacts is about NEW shared surface area that must be created exactly once. See references/plan-assembly.md.

name.description: Canonical identifier of the artifact, e.g. 'Readiness::ScenarioHistoryEntry', 'Types::Readiness::EditPlan', 'factory :readiness_scenario'.

kind.description: What sort of shared surface this is. Drives the phantom-reference check in validate-plan.js.

owner_story.description: The single story that CREATES this artifact. Must be in this wave or an earlier one. Should live in a blocking parallel group so it lands before its reusers dispatch.

reused_by.description: Stories that consume the artifact. They must reuse it verbatim — never re-create. None may be in an earlier wave than owner_story (a reuser can't precede its creator).

canonical_representation.description: The agreed shape, resolved BEFORE dispatch, that all consumers must honor. This is where a contested domain-noun representation is pinned, e.g. 'library scenario: account-less, identified by a draft_graph JSON flag, NO account_id column'. Lifted into every Coder dispatch for the owner and reusers.

repo.description: Name of the repo this artifact lives in (must match a `name` in repos.json).

end_of_wave_actions.description: Actions the orchestrator runs in the wave worktree after all stories merge but BEFORE the wave merges into main and BEFORE Phase B gates run. Used for codegen (regenerate GraphQL schema, db schema dump, manifest aggregation). Failures of these actions block the wave (status: blocked, no fix loop) — see references/execution-protocol.md.

name.description: Short identifier used in commit messages (`End-of-wave: <name>`) and orchestrator logs.

command.description: Shell command to run. Often a one-liner referencing repos.json[].regenerate_commands.

repo.description: Optional — if set, command runs from the repo's path within the wave worktree. If omitted, runs from the wave worktree root.

run_in.description: Where to run the command. Default `wave_worktree` keeps end-of-wave changes in the wave branch before it merges into main.

shared_registry_files.description: Files multiple stories in this wave must each modify (registries / aggregators / index files / locale catalogs / route maps / GraphQL schema entry points). Excluded from the per-task `file_paths` conflict check — instead reconciled by `end_of_wave_actions[]` (kind: merge or regenerate). Without this concept, every story touching such a file would force a serial wave.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the shared file, e.g. 'app/graphql/schema/query.rb' or 'src/i18n/locales/en.ts'.

repo.description: Name of the repo this file lives in (must match a `name` in repos.json).

kind.description: append: each story appends a clean section that the orchestrator concatenates. merge: a script reconciles separate fragments (declared in merge_strategy or regenerated_by). regenerate: derived from sources — the regenerated_by command rebuilds the file at end-of-wave.

merge_strategy.description: Free-form instruction or script ref the orchestrator uses to reconcile this file when kind is `merge`. e.g. 'concatenate fragments alphabetically by feature name'.

regenerated_by.description: Shell command that regenerates this file from sources, run in the wave worktree at end-of-wave when kind is `regenerate` or `merge`. Often a one-liner referencing repos.json[].regenerate_commands.

stories.description: Stories in this wave that touch this file (informational; populated by the planner from each story's touches_registry_files).

post_wave_validation.description: Automated checks to run after the wave completes to confirm correctness

check.description: Human-readable description of what is being verified

command.description: Shell command to execute for this validation

expect.description: Expected output or exit condition, e.g. '0', 'PASS', '200'

steps.description: Walk steps for the journey. Step shape MUST be kept in sync with story-schema.json $defs.chromeMcpStep — both schemas describe the same chrome_mcp walk.

critical_path.description: Ordered list of stories on the critical path. Delays here delay the entire plan.

story_id.description: Story ID on the critical path

blocks.description: The next story this one blocks on the critical path (omit for the final story)

reason.description: Why this story is on the critical path

parallel_strategy.description: Configuration for how parallel execution is managed

max_concurrent_agents.description: Maximum number of agents running in parallel at any time

file_conflict_rules.description: Rules that restrict which agent/group may touch certain files to prevent conflicts

pattern.description: Glob or regex pattern matching the protected files, e.g. 'db/schema.rb'

exclusive_to.description: Parallel group ID or domain that exclusively owns these files

reason.description: Why this exclusivity rule is needed

worktree_strategy.description: How git worktrees are allocated. per_parallel_group = one worktree per group; per_story = one worktree per story.

preflight_requirements.description: Human-gated work that must be completed before specific waves can run. Examples: enabling AWS Bedrock model access, creating LaunchDarkly feature flags, provisioning a database, granting production credentials, populating seed data. Distinct from `risks` (which are probabilistic) — these are deterministic blockers. The orchestrator runs each requirement's `verification` as a Phase B precondition for any wave it blocks; a failing verification produces a wave status of `blocked` (infrastructure-class, not fix-loop).

description.description: What the human needs to do, in actionable terms.

owner.description: Who is responsible for completing this requirement (role or team name).

verification.description: Executable check the orchestrator runs to confirm the requirement is met. Mirrors the DOD validation methods so the same machinery applies.

command.description: String for curl/test_suite/file_exists; array of chrome_mcp steps for chrome_mcp method (same shape as DOD validation in story-schema.json).

expect.description: Expected result (HTTP code, output match, etc.).

blocks_waves.description: Wave IDs that cannot start until this requirement is verified. Use the wave's `id` from `plan.waves[]`.

documentation_url.description: Optional link to runbook, internal docs, or vendor portal explaining how to fulfill this requirement.

notes.description: Free-form notes — for example, lead time required, dependencies on other teams, or known gotchas.

risks.description: Identified risks that could affect plan execution

category.description: Risk category. external_dependency = third-party API/service risk. complexity = underestimated technical challenge. integration = cross-service integration risk. timing = schedule or sequencing risk.

impact.description: Severity of impact if the risk materialises

affected_stories.description: Story IDs that would be affected if this risk materialises
